cl: What is AI? History, narrow vs general AI
Machine learning basics (supervised, unsupervised, reinforcement)
Large language models (LLMs): transformers, training, inference
Vibe coding definition & philosophy (flow, intuition, rapid iteration)
Cognitive load reduction & creative state maintenance
When to use AI assistance vs manual coding
Prompt anatomy: system, user, assistant messages
Zero‑shot, few‑shot, chain‑of‑thought prompting
Context window management & token economy
Code generation strategies: step‑by‑step, test‑driven, self‑healing
Integrating AI into IDE (VS Code, JetBrains plugins)
Version control with AI‑generated code (review, merge, attribution)
AI‑assisted debugging: error interpretation, stack trace analysis
Automated test generation & property‑based testing
Refactoring suggestions & technical debt reduction
GitHub Copilot: features, configuration, best practices
Cursor IDE: AI native editor, composer, chat
ChatGPT / Claude API usage for coding tasks
Local models (Ollama, Llama.cpp) for privacy
Prompt injection, data leakage & safe handling of secrets
Code plagiarism, licensing & AI output ownership
Bias in AI‑generated code & mitigations
Building a vibe coding project from scratch
Team collaboration and AI‑assisted pair programming

units.n: What is AI? History & Core Definitions
units.nt: Covers origins (Dartmouth 1956), narrow vs general vs super AI, symbolic AI vs machine learning, modern resurgence; excludes deep technical details of neural networks.

units.n: Machine Learning Basics: Supervised, Unsupervised, Reinforcement
units.nt: Covers training, features, labels, classification vs regression, clustering, reward signals; excludes mathematical derivations (gradient descent, backprop).

units.n: Large Language Models (LLMs): Transformers & Training
units.nt: Covers attention mechanism, pre‑training and fine‑tuning, autoregressive generation; excludes architecture hyperparameters and scaling laws.

units.n: Inference: Tokens, Temperature, Top‑p, Logits
units.nt: Covers tokenization, sampling strategies, controlling randomness, repetition penalties; excludes beam search variants.

units.n: Vibe Coding Definition & Philosophy
units.nt: Covers flow state, intuitive coding, rapid prototyping, reducing friction, embracing AI as co‑pilot; excludes project management methodologies.

units.n: Cognitive Load Reduction & Creative State Maintenance
units.nt: Covers strategies to stay in flow, avoiding context switching, using AI for boilerplate and scaffolding; excludes psychological research depth.

units.n: When to Use AI vs Manual Coding
units.nt: Covers trade‑offs: speed vs learning, debugging complexity, security‑sensitive code, legacy systems; excludes decision matrices.

units.n: Setting Up the Vibe Coding Environment
units.nt: Covers IDE choice (Cursor, VS Code), notification blocking, ambient music, keyboard‑centric workflow; excludes hardware recommendations.

units.n: Prompt Anatomy: System, User, Assistant Messages
units.nt: Covers structuring conversation, role prompting, instructions vs examples; excludes API implementation details.

units.n: Zero‑shot, Few‑shot & Chain‑of‑Thought Prompting
units.nt: Covers providing examples, reasoning steps, intermediate outputs; excludes advanced techniques like tree‑of‑thoughts.

units.n: Context Window Management & Token Economy
units.nt: Covers token counting, truncation strategies, relevant context selection, multi‑turn summarization; excludes fine‑tuning context extension.

units.n: Iterative Refinement: From Rough to Polished Code
units.nt: Covers asking for corrections, pointing out mistakes, regenerating with changed temperature; excludes formal verification.

units.n: Code Generation Strategies: Step‑by‑Step, Test‑Driven, Self‑Healing
units.nt: Covers incremental generation, writing tests first, using error feedback to fix code; excludes full TDD theory.

units.n: Integrating AI into IDE (VS Code, JetBrains)
units.nt: Covers plugins, inline suggestions, chat panels, keyboard shortcuts; excludes IDE internals.

units.n: Version Control with AI‑Generated Code
units.nt: Covers commit messages generated by AI, reviewing AI diffs, attributing contributions, avoiding merge conflicts; excludes Git internals.

units.n: Documentation & Comments Generation
units.nt: Covers prompting for docstrings, README generation, inline comments, API documentation; excludes Sphinx/JSDoc specifics.

units.n: AI‑Assisted Debugging: Error Interpretation & Stack Trace Analysis
units.nt: Covers pasting errors to LLM, reading stack traces, asking for likely causes; excludes binary analysis.

units.n: Automated Test Generation & Property‑Based Testing
units.nt: Covers generating unit tests, edge cases, fuzzing with AI; excludes formal verification.

units.n: Refactoring Suggestions & Technical Debt Reduction
units.nt: Covers asking AI to simplify, extract functions, rename variables, detect code smells; excludes full static analysis tools.

units.n: Using AI to Explain Legacy or Obfuscated Code
units.nt: Covers feeding foreign code to AI, requesting plain‑English explanations, identifying patterns; excludes reverse engineering.

units.n: GitHub Copilot: Features, Configuration, Best Practices
units.nt: Covers completions, Copilot Chat, code references, privacy modes; excludes enterprise administration.

units.n: Cursor IDE: AI Native Editor, Composer, Chat
units.nt: Covers Cursor’s tab, AI model switching, context rules, composer multi‑file edits; excludes fork differences vs VS Code.

units.n: ChatGPT / Claude API Usage for Coding Tasks
units.nt: Covers API keys, cost tracking, function calling, message threading; excludes advanced agentic workflows.

units.n: Local Models (Ollama, Llama.cpp) for Privacy
units.nt: Covers running open‑weight models offline, hardware requirements, integration with IDE; excludes model quantization math.

units.n: Prompt Injection, Data Leakage & Safe Handling of Secrets
units.nt: Covers risk of exposing API keys, internal code, user data; mitigation via environment variables, output filtering; excludes advanced adversarial ML.

units.n: Code Plagiarism, Licensing & AI Output Ownership
units.nt: Covers copyright status of generated code, open‑source license compatibility, attribution requirements; excludes legal advice.

units.n: Bias in AI‑Generated Code & Mitigations
units.nt: Covers stereotyped variable names, under‑representation, fairness in automation; review strategies; excludes algorithmic fairness metrics.

units.n: Environmental Impact & Sustainable Vibe Coding
units.nt: Covers energy costs of large models, efficient prompting, using local smaller models; excludes carbon footprint models.

units.n: Building a Full‑Stack Vibe Coding Project (Scaffolding to Deploy)
units.nt: Covers using AI to generate boilerplate, iteratively add features, debug, and deploy; end‑to‑end demonstration; excludes large‑scale architecture.

units.n: Team Collaboration & AI‑Assisted Pair Programming
units.nt: Covers sharing prompts, curating AI outputs, code review with AI, synchronous vs asynchronous; excludes organizational change management.

units.n: Reflection & Next Steps: Evolving with AI Tooling
units.nt: Covers keeping up with new models, building personal prompt libraries, contributing to open‑source AI coding tools; excludes career advice.
